be provided with completion documents.
10. INSULATION
10.1 SCOPE
The scope of this section comprises the supply and application of insulation conforming
to these specifications.
10.2 MATERIAL
Insulation material shall be Closed Cell Elastomeric Nitrile Rubber with UV
resistant metal finishing on one side.
Thermal conductivity of insulation material shall not exceed 0.035 W/(m.K) at
mean temperature of 0 C as per EN 12667.
Moisture Diffusion Resistance Factor or value of laminated insulation material
Water Absorption by Volume of insulation material shall be < 0.2% as per ASTM C
1763 / ASTM C 209 for the base foam.
The base insulation material shall have fire performance such that it passes
Class 1 as per BS476 Part 7 for surface spread of flame and also passes Fire
Fire category
as per 1991 Building Regulations (England & Wales) and the Building Standards
(Scotland) Regulations 1990.
Density of laminated insulation material shall be between 50 to 70 Kg/m3.
Density of base foam insulation material shall be between 40 to 55 Kg/m3.
The Tensile strength of the base foam with covering >1.5 MPa (32mm) in MD &
>0.4 MPa (32mm) in CD
The Elongation of the base foam with covering >40% (32mm) in MD & >38%
(32mm) in CD
The Tear strength of the base foam with covering >38 KN/m (32mm) in MD & 27
KN/m (32mm) in CD
The insulation material shall be dust and fibre free.
The insulation material shall be formaldehyde free.
The insulation material shall be CFC & HCFC free.
The insulation material shall withstand maximum surface temperature of +85°C
and minimum surface temperature of 0°C as per EN 14706.
The base insulation material shall have ODP (Ozone Depletion Potential) and
GWP (Global Warming Potential) of Zero.
Covering material shall be of multiple layered laminate of polymeric material
reinforced with scrim with a special UV protection.
Covering material shall be non metallic.
Covering material shall provide mechanical resistance with an excellent aesthetic
look.
Covering material shall be mat finish.
Covering material shall be of 380 ± 20 GSM as per ASTM D 646.
Covering material shall be of 380 400 microns as per ASTM E 252
Covering material shall have the tensile strength of >5 MPa in MD & CD as per
ASTM D 638
Consultant: VMS Engineering and Design Services Private Limited Page 76
Covering material shall have good puncture and tear resistance.
Covering material shall have good UV resistance as per ISO 4892-2 Method A
tested for 1000 Hrs.
Covering material shall withstand maximum surface temperature of +75 °C and
minimum surface temperature of -25 °C as per EN 14706.
Covering material shall be installed with 30mm 50mm overlaps on all horizontal
and longitudinal joints. Joints along the material length shall be
installed facing downward. The joints shall be secured with 50 mm 75 mm
tapes. Manufacturer recommended adhesive can be used for flat sheets, or
where restricted access prevents use of rivets. All joints and overlaps must be
finished with manufacturer recommended PSA Tape.
For fittings of an unusual shape, or large size, the covering material should be cut
and installed as per traditional metal cladding.
Insulation thickness values will be selected as per following selection table.
Insulation
Service Pipe diameter Thickness
(mm)
15mm 80mm 32
Chilled water system pipe 100mm 500mm 51
600mm 1200mm 75
Medium water system pipe 25 mm 350mm 32
Process water system pipe 25 mm 350 mm 32
Ducting insulation thickness shall be as per table below.
Ducting position Insulation Thickness
SA duct 32mm
Return air system 25mm
10.3 PUMP INSULATION
Chilled water pump shall be insulated to the same thickness as the pipe to which they
are connected and application shall be same as above. Care shall be taken to apply
insulation in a manner as to allow the dismantling of pumps without damaging the
insulation.
10.4 SHELL INSULATION
The chiller shells shall be factory insulated in accordance with
standards.
10.5 CHILLED WATER EXPANSION TANK INSULATION
Cold water tank, and chilled water expansion tank shall be insulated as
standard.
Consultant: VMS Engineering and Design Services Private Limited Page 77
10.6 ACOUSTIC LINING OF MECHANICAL ROOMS
Two walls and ceiling of air conditioning plant room and air handling unit rooms may be
provided with acoustic lining. Material shall be processed from Nitrile rubber foam.
The material should be fiber free. The density of the same shall be 140-180 Kg/m3. The
material should have thermal conductivity not exceeding 0.05 W/Mk. The maximum
surface temperature of material shall withstand is 1050C. and minimum temp shall be -
200C. Thickness shall be as specified. The material should confirm to class 1 rating for
surface spread of flame as per BS 476
